findings.title: Deletion paths built from untrusted RunMeta.run_id can escape .ritual/runs/
findings.scenario: A meta file containing "run_id": "../../important" makes clean delete ../../important.jsonl outside the runs dir.
findings.action: Run ids for deletion are derived only from discovered filenames; every deletion target is asserted to resolve inside runs_dir.

findings.title: history::load_all silently skips malformed metas, so those runs are invisible to cleanup and the directory keeps growing
findings.scenario: A corrupt old.meta.json next to a multi-gigabyte old.jsonl is never selected for pruning; .ritual/runs/ growth is not actually bounded.
findings.action: Enumeration switched to filename-based grouping over the runs dir; malformed and orphaned groups are classified and prunable.

findings.title: Plan deletes only .jsonl + .meta.json, orphaning .request.json and .status sidecars; live runs need a liveness check before pruning meta-less groups
findings.scenario: Crashed or in-flight launches leave sidecars without a parseable meta that are never returned by load_all and accumulate forever; an age-only heuristic could delete the archive of a still-running >24h agent while it is being written.
findings.action: All four artifact types are treated as one group; groups are classified with the existing runner::run_state() (live pid in .status => never prune; Vanished => prunable). Claude's initial 24h age-threshold rebuttal was withdrawn after Codex pushed back and run_state() was found to already provide pid liveness.

findings.title: Pruning any prefix of the hash chain makes verify-log fail permanently for all retained and future runs, contradicting 'acceptable, documented'
findings.scenario: After pruning, the oldest retained chained run's prev link no longer equals GENESIS; verify_log reports Broken forever, and new runs keep chaining onto the retained head so the log never self-heals.
findings.action: clean refuses to delete any chained run unless --allow-chain-break is passed, with an explicit permanence warning exercised by --dry-run too; risk note corrected. A checkpoint/rebased-chain redesign of verify_log was agreed by both models to be an open decision deferred to the user, not silently adopted or dropped.

findings.title: Pair deletion is non-atomic with no failure policy; partial failure makes verify-log report tampering
findings.scenario: Archive deletion succeeds but meta deletion fails (permissions/IO); history still lists the run and verify_log reads the missing archive as empty bytes, reporting a content-hash mismatch as tampering.
findings.action: Meta-first deletion order so partial failure leaves only an orphan group that the next clean collects; continue past per-run failures, report each, exit nonzero. Codex's rename-to-trash staging suggestion was rebutted as creating a second GC problem; Codex accepted meta-first ordering with the continue/report/nonzero condition, which was adopted.

findings.title: Interaction between --keep and stage-protected runs is ambiguous
findings.scenario: With 100 runs, --keep 50, and 20 referenced runs among the oldest 50, the algorithm retains 70 runs while a user may expect 50 total; the retention target is not met as understood.
findings.action: Semantics defined as additive: protected runs never consume keep slots and total retained may exceed keep; documented in CLI help and tested for protected runs inside and outside the keep window, including duplicate references.

findings.title: Proposed tests omit the command's principal safety and failure contracts
findings.scenario: Dry-run mutation, keep=0, malformed metas, orphan artifacts, live vs dead status files, run_id path escape, partial deletion failure, chained histories, protected-run placement, and missing state.json are all untested, so every failure scenario above can ship undetected.
findings.action: Test list in the plan expanded to cover all of these cases explicitly.
